evals.prompt:
/project-spec

We're a 6-person startup building a B2B SaaS for construction subcontractors to track change orders and lien deadlines. MVP in 4 months. Stack idea: Next.js 15 on Vercel, Postgres on Neon, Clerk for auth, Stripe for subscriptions. Need multi-tenant isolation, admin vs field-user roles, and exports to CSV for their accountants. Compliance: nothing HIPAA. Integrations: maybe QuickBooks later—not MVP. Team is TypeScript-heavy, one person knows a bit of Python. Success = first 10 paying GCs using it weekly.
evals.expected_output: Phased interview with lettered options and [RECOMMENDED] defaults; devil's advocate; 8–10 phase roadmap awaiting approval; then in-memory recursive-refine and two files (constitution + XML spec) only after gates; Phase 8 asks for proceed/revise/stop before any implementation.
expectations: First substantive reply either asks about existing project-spec.md/project-constitution.md or states none found, then begins structured interview
Interview batches use a), b), c) style options with at least one [RECOMMENDED] per question
Assistant does not create application source code, install dependencies, or scaffold a repo before explicit user permission matching Phase 8
Roadmap has between 8 and 10 numbered phases with titles and dependencies before file writes
Devil's advocate section presents 3–5 numbered risks/challenges before asking how to address them

evals.prompt:
project spec — internal only

My manager wants a small web dashboard for our ops team to replay failed Kafka consumer messages from a dead-letter topic into a staging sink (not prod). Java/Spring at work, deploy on our k8s cluster, behind corporate SSO (Okta). Data is PII-light but still internal. I need a spec I can hand to another engineer; no mobile app. Timeline ~6 weeks part-time.
evals.expected_output: Archetype leans internal tool / data-adjacent; interview covers audit, prod write safety, SSO, idempotency; same gates and deliverables as eval 1.
expectations: Questions reflect internal-tool or pipeline concerns (e.g. audit, prod access, deployment boundary)
Waits for user answers between phases rather than inventing all requirements in one shot
Final artifacts target project root as project-constitution.md and project-spec.md with the prescribed structures

evals.prompt: Use /project-spec for this idea: cross-platform React Native app + Node API for a local gym's class booking and push reminders. They have ~800 members, one location, budget is tight. Owner wants Apple Pay + Google Pay eventually; start with card on file via Stripe. Offline: nice-to-have for viewing schedule only. I'm solo dev, never shipped RN to both stores.
evals.expected_output: Mobile + backend archetype questions; timeline realism and store submission called out in devil's advocate; innovation step may propose something small; recursive-refine and XML spec.
expectations: Interview includes mobile-relevant dimensions (offline, push, sync, or store constraints) in at least one batch
Explicit approval checkpoint for the roadmap (approved / changes) before drafting final specs
project-spec.md uses the XML template shape from Phase 7 (project_specification root and core child tags)
